content: I've been trying for well over an hour to log into the app after changing my password on my account. I continuously get the error that it's a server issue and thus cannot log in. I'm frustrated and confused, as I love to use steam. I had it on my phone to share the games I play with my friends on the go, and now am unable to. I feel this is a bug rather than the end all be all, but I do hope it's fixed.

Downloaded today and couldn't login. I have had the same password for way too long, so even though I knew it was correct I updated it. Still didn't work, so I double-checked my username, yep it was right... I even went through the whole process a third time to copy and paste from my notes app the password to MAKE SURE I input it correctly and still nada. Infuriating. I've tried to download this app a couple times over the years and can never login.

If it works, it's good for doing Steam browsing & shopping. However, as of yesterday, the app stops working entirely after the first log-in, only reopening to a blank screen. This is an issue that I have seen others report as well. If you download it, never try to change your password or log out. While it won't result in losing access to your steam account, you won't be able to confirm any log-ins, trade offers, etc. unless you reinstall the whole app & move your authenticator each time.

I never write reviews. But wow. I'm actually impressed by how unusable this app is. Got stuck on the steam guard. Tried inputting the code sent to my email, and it randomly puts them into the box. Can't delete, can only try, helplessly to input in the correct order. When it finally happens, it errors out and you have to start from the previous screen anyways.

I didn't believe it was possible, but they actually made the app worse with the latest update. Using the store is much slower and more laggy compared to the previous version. Suddenly I have a bunch of "notifications" just to bombard me with game sales. Even if the game is on my wishlist, I don't need a notification for every sale. Worst of all though, is the removable of the friends list from the app. I don't care that I can't chat anymore, but not being able to even see who is online is awful.

They gutted and ruined their app and made it so you now have to download 2 separate apps just to try to get the same functionality that this one app used to do just fine. Except neither app does any of the things as well as the old one did. EDIT: Now, additionally, to make a purchase while already logged in, it's asking me to log in again. I do so, and after jumping through all of their 2FA hoops, it just asks me to log in again in an endless loop. It also won't let me contact support.

Old app was just fine and worked exactly like the pc store version. Now, its slow asf. The full webpages don't even display. For example, ill go to a workshop page for a mod for a game. The text is perfectly fine, but the images are half unseeable and you cannot scroll the page left or right. Its frustrating and not only that whenever i try to use the website instead it autodirects me back to the app. If i wanna use the desktop version, let me....

When it works, it's great. But it's even buggier than the old Steam app. Nearly every time I open it, I have to manually kill and restart Steam because it's in a bad state. They spent all their effort on style, and cut every QA corner they possibly could have. It's so bad I used Tasker to create a shortcut to kill & relaunch Steam with one press. EDIT: more than 2 years later... still true.

This is a joke, right? Everything about the app is worse. I've been trying to give it a chance but you can't even maneuver through the interface without in immense amount of redundancy. It'll take 4-6 presses to get to the store homepage, and you'll see the same page several times trying to get there. Horrible experience. Constantly redirected off of discussion pages and losing my text. My cart on the app and on desktop aren't synced for some reason and the community market is awful.

This app is a complete waste of time. I would have NEVER downloaded it if steam hadn't made it mandatory for me to sell my inventory items on the marketplace. Setting it up took me three days to do- because there's a bug where you input sms codes for verification, however everytime I entered a code, it gave me a #2 error code. Ended up getting my account locked, and now I can't sell for two weeks. What a garbage, garbage app.

This app bricked my account! 1000s of dollars in games gone. And steam doesn't care. I can prove it's my account. But because of their 2FA policy, forced down our throats, they refuse to give me my games! This is a shining example of why you should NEVER buy anything through steam. And why we need to bring back physical game carts. Or else, it's only a matter of time until you lose everything. If you can't hold it, you don't own it!

Hey, I just downloaded Steam and ran into some issues while trying to create an account. I kept getting stuck in a loop of email verification, and despite verifying it several times, it only recognized the email as already in use twice. This has prevented me from creating a username and password. Maybe I'm doing something wrong but whatever I'm doing just isn't working.

Terrible update. I refuse to have two apps for one platform. Searching games in one and chatting in the other.. if either app actually worked properly. I am really disappointed and removing Steam mobile altogether. Things can wait until I get home to my pc. They ruined a familiar, easy to use, fully functional, all-in-one app that we all once loved.

If you say and claim fixing any login issues then your lying. I experience them both on the mobile and PC apps. On the mobile app it does an infinite and repeating "email verified" yet it doesn't work(This is for account creation) because it just resets account creation. On the PC, Captcha glitches and says it doesn't work and that's been an issue for years. I hate that these bugs seem to pile up ontop of one other, and I am not able to make an account whatever way possible. Please fix these.

Updating my review from a 2 star to a 1 star. The app is truly awful. As well as being a general buggy mess when browsing, it failed when I ordered my Steam Deck causing a stuck pending transaction for £349 that I had to call the bank to clear. I also tried to buy games (forgetting my previous ordeal). The same thing happened again. Steam told me "the 3D authentication process has failed" and that is true - because the app failed. I am now out of pocket with no options but to wait and hope.

Tried to purchase a game through the app using PayPal and it would just constantly fail to even login. Just open an external browser window instead of trying to open everything in the app. The in-app browser is terrible and fails most of the time when attempting to intercept links to games on Steam. More often than not it gets stuck on one and won't open any others without being completely closing the app and trying again. Just use an external browser so that things can actually work. Thanks.

Will NOT open, no matter how long I let it try to. The moment I click on the icon, I get stuck on a grey loading screen with the text "loading parental settings" in tiny black letters (i have no parental settings enabled." I was able to use the app a small handful of times before this issue began, and I remember it being a cumbersome and frustrating expierence. All my trades are forced to be delayed because I "haven't acessed the app in a week," despite the fact I CANNOT access it.

App is broken. It is clear that Steam wants to sell Steam Decks, which is fine, but they seem to have abandoned the app in the process. When it is a chore just to find a way to go back to what you were previously viewing, and it says that the PayPal you have used for purchases for YEARS no longer is valid, even when I reset it, then there is a problem

Used to work well. I could get my authenticator code either as a notification or through a widget. Now I would be forced to open the app and use a qr code (not happening). Or select the option to use the old code system. But wait, the code option doesn't work. Instead it tries to make you approve or deny the request based upon your IP location. TLDR: logging into desktop is a nightmare and you can't log out of app.

It took me 20 mins to send a simple message to a friend because of the new lay out. The friends list is buried and when I do finally get to the messages, it loads a completely different tab and then it's so squished I can't type or click. Why fix something if it isn't broken? I was excited to switch from Xbox but this is just getting to be too much of a hassle.

Slowest app on Google Play. If you have even the most minor interruption in your connection, becomes completely unusable. Most of the app is just a browser for the Steam website, but worse than a regular browser in every way. Doesn't even let you chat, that's a separate app! It's easier, faster, and more convenient to use the website. Steam requires you to install the app for 2FA and marketplace stuff. Worst part of Steam hands down. Absolutely miserable.

It wants me to access a menu to verify a code to change my password, but I can't access the menu without logging in, which I can't do because I don't remember my password... Why can't I just enter the code I see in the app like before??? How this made it through testing is beyond me. This is critical functionality. App is literally useless currently.

I have really wanted to play a few games that I've only seen on steam. However, I've been trying to create an account for the last hour through my PC and my phone. Nothing is working and I cannot create an account. Captcha keeps saying my responses are invalid. This is absolutely ridiculous. You have someone wanting to pay for games and your site, servers, and software won't allow them to create an account.

I can't access my wishlist, I can't add anything to my wishlist because it says I'm not signed in (I uninstalled the app, reinstalled it, signed back in, and it still won't let me do anything), tried to load my profile, doesn't work. Says there is a server error, try to reload, says the same thing again. Idk what happened, the app was just fine before and all of a sudden it's just not allowing me to do anything with my account. Would enjoy them rolling back the update cause it's unusable.

Trying to communicate with anyone in "support," is a byzantine migraine. Dive the app update, the app has both gained a ton of functionality, and lost it. I honestly thought I could rely on the app, and the Steam Deck to manage my library, but they've kinda made it impossible. I can't really do anything other than spend money, and I can't leave feedback, or talk to anyone, or accomplish anything I need to on my phone, or on the Steam Deck with a Bluetooth keyboard and mouse.

Installed after the new update and instantly regretted it. The only reason I'm still trying to bend it to my will is the app spares me the seemingly daily verification of login dance that I get with Chrome mobile. Half the time, links to Steam (e.g. to a game's store page) are not resolved correctly and you're just taken to a default page (Store main usually). I can't do something as simple as see my own recent gaming activity. Some of the menu items in the account overview view also don't work

They stripped out functionality for seemingly no reason. Previously you could do pretty much anything you wanted in this app. Browse store, buy stuff, talk with friends, etc. Now they have gotten rid of the ability to chat with friends (you need a separate app for that), and they got rid of rid of the nice mobile authentication widget. Thanks for making your app much, much worse you dirty heathens.

Poorly designed UI, extremely slow/laggy, won't load my Library. The old app worked fine. They took what wasn't broken and "fixed" it, and the result is as bad as you might expect. Just give us the old app back. Edit: It's currently the Black Friday/"Autumn" Sale and the app's store page won't load, just keeps saying "There was a problem loading this web content" with no solution I can find. This app is literally making me less likely to spend money on Steam because I'm simply unable to.

When booting up my PC today, the Steam app asked for my user ID & password (something I haven't had to to enter in YEARS). Obviously can't remember, so clicked the "Help, I can't sign in" link. It told me to open the steam app on my phone, and scan a QR code. So I open the steam app on my phone, and it asks for my user ID & password. There is literally no way to get around it!

It is quite literally impossible to create an account! I need to input my email, and then confirm my email, but when I confirm my email, I'm taken to the sign in screen, in which I don't have an account to sign in with! Trying to create an account online is also impossible, as no matter how you do the captha, it always fails me! This is such an incredible waste of time.

I literally can't sign into my account on mobile anymore. It keeps asking me to authenticate with a steamguard code, but when I go to type it in the letters keep jumbling around and the keyboard keeps dissapearing. I can't find anywhere to contact steam support. The email listed on their site sends a response back saying it's no longer monitored. None of the self help questions fit my situation, and I can't find where to open a ticket. Used to love steam, but now I can't even open it.

Terrible, can't sign up on your website so I installed the app. Get to your app to see only a sign up screen. I've authenticated my email twice now, everything goes through but there's no way to go past that point so I can't continue actually creating my account for Steam. As of now I'm left with two attempts on the same email, with no page I can manually get to, and no automatic prompt system in place. About to try a third time but I'm pretty sure we know how that's going to go

I, over 2 years ago, had the same email verification issue on account creation that people are still talking about recently in the reviews. Contacting customer support in steam is impossible. Trying to return a game is impossible. This is honestly one of the worst run operations I've ever experienced. The only issue I haven't had on my PC is buying and downloading video games.

It's a garbage site with no support. Have been using it for years and have never been happy with it. The website won't let me log in, even after verifying my account and changing my password, so it prompted me to download the app. Now Steam is on my PC, my laptop, and my phone, and I can't log into any of them. I get to their Captcha anti robot and get stuck in an infinite loop of selecting pics of bicycles and busses. There is no way to contact Steam support without already being logged in.

It's pretty bad. You actually cannot buy anything with PayPal on the app and has been an issue for over a year now with no plans to fix it. Then they removed all the chat functionality to another app that doesn't actually work either, so this all's only use is for Steam Guard. Don't even bother to browse games, you can't buy 'em! Might as well just stick to your mobile's web browser.

Much improved! The ui is nice and things seem more optimized and with less stuttering. Overall, always room for improvement, but I think it runs well. 4/5 EDIT 10/13/22 Steam recently changed it's UI and it's terrible. It's focus is completely on the store, you have to click at least 2 icons to get to your friends. On top of that, it's just plain slow. If I didn't have to use the app for 2FA I would have uninstalled. 1/5

Currently unusable for steam guard authentication. Very annoyed and won't be holding my breath for an update (as nobody at Valve reads anymore.) Used to be simple lightweight and useful. Steam guard worked fine if a bit annoying how often the code would change often lead to an annoying wait to jump in and play when my limited schedule allowed. QR codes probably won someone a raise. Someone else probably suggested updating mobile app to match the new PC client. New authentication doesn't work.

I bought the Romance of the Three Kingdoms game through Steam. Had a gameplay technical issue that would not resolve. I requested a refund from Steam, and they refused to provide one as it had been over two weeks since initially buying the game (Steam directed me to the game publisher for support, who I didn't hear back from). Have bought a number of games through Steam over the years, now can't say I trust them.

Genuinely the worst sign in/create an account process I've ever seen from any app ever. Sometimes the captcha just doesn't work, and when it does you have to reverify. You get sent an email to verify your account, but when you click on it it takes you to a sign in page and doesn't actually verify your account. This shouldn't be this complicated.

App is slow to load, scrolling is overly sensitive, links to games are broken, sometimes it directs you to the right spot, other times it just takes you to the store home page. Before the store front would take you to interesting places if you kept exploring long enough, I'd spend a good amount of time hunting for hidden gems, now it has become more of a walled garden experience.

I just wanted to sell my steam items without waiting 15 days and verified my listings, only to be stopped by needing the steam mobile authenticator, so I got the app and tried setting it up only for it to go through the whole set up process. Only for it to tell me to set it up again repeatedly, it can't do the one thing I only needed it for. Not like theres any other way to get my items off hold.

With QR code updates to steam guard, someone was easily able to hack into my account, blocking me out and stealing, my personal information, credit card and thousands of dallors worth of in game items. Steam's Guards 2-step mobile varification never alerted me of anyone else logging into my account. Trying to communicate w/ Support to help, was almost none existent, extremely slow and very non remorseful about the situation. Steam is an unreliable, untrustworthy game client . Uninstalling steam.

My experience with Steam across all devices has left much to be desired. It's never even let me sign in! For Pete's sake, loosen up your reCAPTCHA and let me sign in! I've reached out for support multiple times to no response other than the automated "we got your message," and I'm done. This all just sucks because I've heard a lot of good things about this company, but I can't get even a word back. Do better.

I don't like having to get a second app for chat. I liked how the old app opened up to my friends list right away. Reason being my main use of the steam app was to see who's online. Now it's a few clicks to get to the friends list. I can only hope they revert back to the old version. I don't want to delete this and rely on Discord to see who's playing what. But if it stays this way I don't see me using the Steam app.

This app is horrible. It doesn't work and 75% of the time it says there was an error loading the page and I have 1gb fiber internet speeds so it's not my connection. It just doesn't work. Steam chat app is better and works for being able to message your friends but that's it. Steam on pc is amazing but the steam app for your phone is horrible and they need to fix it! It only works 25% of the time, if at all.

The older version of this app was bad. This updated version is unusable. Discussions have to be backed out of using the back button. So, if you frequent the forums, it becomes a huge hassle getting to the next one. The app doesn't remember settings. If you click through a content warning and press remember this choice, the app does not remember. Content warnings on every page. It's difficult to chat with friends. You're frequently logged out over and over within seconds.

News bulletin do not I repeat. Do not have your phone or device plugged in to the computer. That you are currently using for your steam games, etc. While having this app on it. All anyone needs is your IP address. If you currently have your phone charging via your PC, they have access to your steamguard and turn can empty your steam wallet, delete games, et cetera. I had a little market transaction from a nice person from China. That basically bought items that they had up for sale using mycash

The constant stupid crashing. I swear i buy a game turn around and all the sudden im signed out of my steam. Login info is all the sudden wrong . Lengthy and laaggy support options that ended with my account having to be "unlocked" by some idiot on the other side of the world. Log back in just to have same issues. Authenticator is a joke "failed to get qr info" .. do not download if you want to save your time. A whole night with not one game downloaded.

Made a steam account over 10 years ago and have never been able to play on it because the computer I was using auto-filled somebody else's email and even though I can login, it requires email confirmation. I've discussed this many times will steam support but they refused to help me get access to my account or even to get my name back. 😒

Random mess of a code. Logged in. Was getting blank inactive pages, so i could never confirm trades. Googled. Tried to re-log-in as an option. No luck. Just as my later login attempts. Reinstalled, device restart, cache cleared - nothing helped. I put my data, but "Log-in" button just does nothing. My phone is old. But the app page says my phone is ok to run steam app. And here I am.. Authenticate your arse, valve.

Only thing that consistently works on the app is the store page and even that doesn't seem work half the time. Don't waste your time and system space just get on your desktop and go through steam there. Most of the features available on desktop are not here. Unable to access saved favorites and if I open a guide or discussion on steam from a web browser or messaging service it'll reset the app and take you to your library instead.

This app used to be 15MB in size and had chat, 2 factor authentication, and even the store all in a single app. Now, it's split into 2 different apps that take up about 300MB of storage, are more cumbersome to use, and crash constantly. Way to take a perfect example of a great, simple, useful, and functional app and turn it into such bloatware that I'm seriously considering disabling 2 factor authentication just so I can uninstall it.

Functionally useless app. I can't tell you how many sales this app has cost steam. I keep a wishlist for sales but this app has never successfully loaded a page so I could purchase the games. I would have bought a bunch of these but the app just doesn't do the one thing it really should. Steam... You're losing money you could get if this app actually worked properly.

Honestly, I'm not sure how the semi-recent overhaul of the Steam app ever got pushed, considering Valve used to be known for their really good quality assurance in delivering some of the best and most polished PC games of all time. Literally everything in the app is bugged (which wasn't before), minus the authentication portion itself, which works pretty well most of the time. I think the app just needs to be fully reverted until they can push the overhaul without all of the bugs.

New app is horrible. I've used the steam app for years. Made lots of purchases through it and messaged friends. Now I can't message like I could and nothing loads. I wish I could have kept the old one. Worked well. If this doesn't change soon I'm just uninstalling it. I have no idea why they did this. It's BAD. Edit: Over a month later and still no update or fixes. If anything I'd say it may have gotten worse somehow. It's just completely broken. Done with this garbage. Uninstalling.

After I buy a game from Steam they lock my account and give me a excuse of the game that you buy was fraudulent, then they don't have phone number to contact them directly you have to write them thru email and they take there sweet time answering sow far they take 1 1/2 days to answer any questions and/or ask you for more info and then the clock reset again. I'm a soldier on a deployment this is one of the few was I can actually have fun and even that is been put to the test. Thanks for noting.

Another big tech company making yet another bad UI overhaul instead of actually executing on the deliverables that brought their customers to their platform in the first place. The new app is slower, less secure, less user friendly, mines more data and makes the only services most people got it for (steam guard and friends list) more difficult and more tedious than ever. Worse it loads all steam community links from google search with in-app steam browser (which is an awful browser!!).

Would grant 0 stars if possible, all the updates have done nothing but render the app unusable for the primary reasons people would use it. I cannot even make a purchase on the app because it wants me to sign in, in the apps, that I'm already signed in on, and doing so takes me to the steam guard page, which then doesn't let me finish the sign in. Chat function doesn't work, which was the primary reason many people even used the app. If it weren't for 2FA, I would uninstall. Revert the Update.

Terrible authentication system. Do not try logging into steam from more than one app per day because it will lock you out of your own account. It will also make you use authentication via email or your phone regardless of if you used the correct password, causing an error that you tried logging in too many times meaning you can't play for SEVERAL HOURS. This is unacceptable.

I struggled with this app for a couple days - tried to install it on an android phone, iPad, brand new chromebook, older chromebook, windows laptop. I finally got the app *sort of* functional, and then it got stuck on steamguard. Not one device could help me get past setting up steamguard, which is ostensibly there to protect registered users. So I can buy whatever I want from the store but I can't actually access any of it because steamguard insists I'm not logged in. Less than useless.

Horrific since launch. Literally never works properly and got even more useless after the split of the steam chat app (which is also just as bad). Frequent crashes on major Samsung hardware and blank pages for things like library. Profile is glorified web browser that doesn't scale properly no matter what phone you're on. It's easier to just get in a web browser and log in that way honestly.

It's better on PC and on several other browsers on mobile than this messed-up app. Some content doesn't properly load, such as previews of screenshots and artwork, the app breaks itself saying "there was a problem loading this content" and "webpage not available" for an excuse, and sometimes it takes forever to open something so I have to reopen the app again. It's really annoying.

Utterly useless. Extremely slow to load any page. Unresponsive, buggy, can't scroll down on some pages without it jumping to top instantly, the swipe navigation you are forced to use skips items, constant crashes, out right crashed my phone on more than one occasion, drops connectivity . Not even going to touch on the login issues. Has been terrible since day one and has only gotten worse.

Steam refusing to allow username changes left me being harassed, so much so that I had to create an entire new Steam account. I lost all the games I bought, all my rewards, and all my achievements - some of which I cannot earn back. I think Steam's username policy is extremely outdated and harmful to players. This review will stay until they change their policy and will fix my account.

It won't let me create an account. After verification I try to create a password and all it says is password has to be 8 characters long. I have tried 8 -12 characters without special characters, with caps, no caps, numbers. I try to get help and this issue isn't addressed in their "wizard" or forum and there is no way obvious way to reach out to a person to fix. Tried on computer, cell and app.

The recent update is garbage. I'm not going to download a whole 'nother app just because the devs wanted to remove chatting from the regular steam app. It was a lot more convenient to have everything in one app and it barely even functions anymore anyways. It crashes and freezes far more often since the update, so DON'T use this app. Just use your computer, at least it works as intended.

Absolute jumbled mess of an app. Want to change your email address? Too bad. Because you have to verify your identity on the app, when you're using the app, and get an error message because you did not confirm your conformations on the app, WHILE YOUR ARE USING THE APP. And 4 texted super secret codes and 2 emailed codes later there is shockingly an issue with the steam servers and try again later...this isn't an app it's a train wreck.

The app has always been an annoyance, but it's only gotten worse. Nowadays, trade confirmations don't let you view the item description anymore. The app often takes literal minutes to respond to anything. Navigating the app has always been confusing, but they have "improved" on that -- it's gotten more confusing. The only redeeming feature of this app is that Steam basically forces you to use it.

So I haven't used my account in awhile and don't have the details wrote down. Tried using the "Forgot username/password" feature to get help remembering it. Only problem is when you try to use that feature Steam give you this message: Please launch the Steam Mobile app on your mobile device, and then select the Confirmations menu option. And the problem is you cannot do that because there is no confirmations menu option. It just goes straight to wanting me to sign-in. You need to fix this!

Update: Using an old version, 2.3.1, fixes the notification issue. Please fix the current version of the app so that it functions as well or better than the old ones versions. Chat push notifications not working on Android 9 Pixel 3 XL 90% of the time. My wife is having the same problems with the same phone and android version. On the rare occasions they work, it is playing the global android notification sound instead of the set default steam sound.

Permanent Grey screen. No idea why. It was working when I last used the app. Now I cannot do anything. Cannot confirm trades. God help me if I ever need to use the app to log into desktop app. Being locked out of my game library because of a faulty program that was billed as improving account security would be a kick in the nuts. Anyway to disable 2-step authentication? I really don't want to rely on this app for access to my 20-year game library.

Recently ordered Valve Index, one for me and one for my wife. Cool, awesome, can't wait. An hour later the order was canceled without a reason given! How hard is it to inform the customer why an order was canceled in the first place? Try contacting customer support - which doesn't exist at all. Navigate menu until I find a place to add details/or ticket. Of course nothing comes back, because it's entirely automated. Refund still hasn't returned to my Bank account so I can't even find an alterna

The best thing about this app is that it "works"...even that's a bit of an overstatement. Loading is slow, frequent crashing is inevitable... It's horrible with account recognition and the user interface feels like your trudging through mud. It is genuinely more infuriating than it is endearing a solid 85% of the time you use it. I don't even want to bother mentioning the support team, for lack of any "support" at all.

Trying to buy something in the app is impossible. Everything is in my cart, then I'm asked to log in to verify my identity. A QR scanner pops up and then after several seconds a box appears to approve my ID and then the QR reader returns. Clicking "Help I can't log in" returns with a "You are already logged in as ****" and the cycle repeats itself. I cannot get past account verification to make a damn purchase.

Manny times doesn't allow login. It show that it was some eror and after couple of times show to try later because it was too many attempts, especially if I dont use steam for couple of days or weeks. Even if everything work from first time it know to take 10-30 minutes for no specific reason. There is no easy and fast way to contact support. If you are interested to use steam be prepared for stress before start any game.

I just want to say that whoever is in charge of the mobile version of Steam needs to be fired on the spot. It is so full of visual and UI bugs, and it hasn't gotten any better. You will have parts of your screen turn grey at random times. Also when you purchase a game on the Steam app, it will show that you have bought a different game than the one you purchased. How do you even mess up an app function that bad? Looking up the website works better than this app. This app is (mostly) unusable.

This is the most difficult thing to ever log into. After you put in your email it send you a code. While typing the code it will mismatch the numbers around so you can never get it correct. Also if you keyboard goes away you have to restart the app and restart the login. This is my 5th time trying to get past just this stage and it is impossible.

This app is awful and so is the support for it. I can sign in but I get an error trying to do most things. When the update for the new layout released I was so excited because it finally looked and felt good to use. But now I can't even add anything to my wishlist or buy anything. The support options online don't help either, there's either no option for this issue, or it makes me open the app which leads to an ERROR. So dumb, I've missed out on so many sales because my computer is packed up.

Got logged out of the mobile app and desktop at the same time, meaning I can't get a token. Literally every time I try to use another way, I get an email saying that "for my security" I can't recover the account via email or phone. I saved a recovery code. It doesn't work.
